skill.name: book-scaffolding
skill.description: Plan and scaffold educational books with clear parts, chapter dependencies, learning outcomes, cognitive load controls, and validation workflows for long-form technical content.
skill.summary: Structure educational books with chapter plans, dependency maps, and learning scaffolds.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, crypto, reconnaissance, and obfuscation patterns, but review found they are almost entirely Markdown or YAML teaching examples and keyword collisions. The Python scripts parse local YAML files and print JSON results; no shell execution, credential access, active network calls, or prompt injection attempt was found. The remaining risk is low because the skill includes local helper scripts and passive external reference URLs.

low_findings.title: Local YAML Parsing Scripts
low_findings.description: The skill includes helper scripts that read a user-provided book outline path and parse YAML before printing JSON analysis. This is expected functionality, but it is still local filesystem access that should run only on trusted outline files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts clearly open a local path supplied on the command line and parse it with yaml.safe_load. I found no write operation, subprocess call, or network transmission in the reviewed script flow.

low_findings.title: Passive External Reference URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are template fields for book repositories, errata pages, forums, and documentation references. They are not fetched by the skill or scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only as YAML template values under supplementary materials and external references. No HTTP client, request library, or URL-fetching code was found.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The many backtick execution findings are Markdown fenced examples, inline formatting, or diagram blocks rather than Ruby or shell execution. They do not execute when the skill is loaded.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative flagged locations are Markdown code fences and educational diagrams. The executable Python files do not import subprocess, call os.system, or use shell backticks.

low_findings.title: Static Sensitive and Crypto Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The scanner matched ordinary educational text such as password hashing, part titles, and structural terminology. I found no credential extraction, weak cryptographic implementation, or Windows SAM database access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose and outline examples, not code that reads secrets or implements cryptography. Keyword search found no credential variables, hashing functions, or sensitive file paths in executable code.

content.value_statement: Large instructional books are hard to organize across chapters, exercises, and learning goals. This skill provides scaffolds, templates, and checks for coherent educational book planning.
actual_capabilities: Creates part and chapter structures for long-form educational books.
Maps prerequisites, dependencies, and reading paths between chapters.
Defines learning outcomes, success criteria, and exercise placement.
Provides YAML templates for book outlines and chapter structures.
Includes Python utilities that validate chapter dependencies and analyze learning flow.
Guides staged planning from book-level outline to chapter-level implementation.
limitations: It does not write complete book manuscripts by itself.
The templates require project-specific titles, audiences, and chapter details.
The helper scripts need PyYAML for YAML parsing.
It does not verify factual accuracy of educational content.

use_cases.title: Design a Technical Book Outline
use_cases.description: Plan parts, chapters, reading paths, and prerequisites before drafting a programming or software book.

use_cases.title: Build a Course Companion Text
use_cases.description: Convert a curriculum into chapters with learning objectives, exercises, assessments, and progression checks.

use_cases.title: Review a Book Structure
use_cases.description: Check whether a draft outline has coherent dependencies, balanced pacing, and clear success criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Book Outline
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new educational book.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use book-scaffolding to create a high-level outline for a book about [topic]. The audience is [audience]. Include parts, chapter themes, prerequisites, and learning outcomes.

prompt_templates.title: Map Chapter Dependencies
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate planning after an outline exists.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use book-scaffolding to review this chapter list and map required, recommended, and optional dependencies. Identify any missing foundation chapters or unclear reading paths.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Chapter Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Use book-scaffolding to design Chapter [number]: [title]. Include learning objectives, section flow, key concepts, examples, exercises, success criteria, and links to earlier chapters.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Full Book Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of a multi-part book plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use book-scaffolding to audit this full book outline for cognitive load, dependency cycles, concept density, exercise balance, and part-to-part narrative continuity.

output_examples.input: Plan a beginner Python book for non-programmers.
output: A seven-part outline with a foundation-first sequence.
Chapter summaries with learning outcomes and prerequisite notes.
Reading paths for complete beginners and faster learners.

output_examples.input: Review this chapter list for dependency problems.
output: A dependency map that separates required, recommended, and optional links.
Warnings for chapters that introduce advanced concepts too early.
Suggestions for reordering or adding bridge chapters.

output_examples.input: Design one chapter about debugging command-line tools.
output: A chapter plan with objectives, sections, examples, practice tasks, and assessment checks.
A cognitive load estimate for each section.
A list of expected reader outcomes by the end of the chapter.

best_practices: Define the target audience and prerequisites before planning chapters.
Use dependency maps before writing detailed lessons.
Validate each chapter against measurable learning outcomes.
anti_patterns: Creating all chapter details before the book narrative is clear.
Adding advanced concepts before the required foundation exists.
Using vague outcomes that cannot be assessed.

faq.question: Can this skill create a complete book manuscript?
faq.answer: No. It plans structure, dependencies, learning goals, and chapter scaffolds for later writing.

faq.question: What kinds of books fit this skill best?
faq.answer: It fits educational, technical, and curriculum-based books with multiple chapters and progressive learning goals.

faq.question: Does it support nontechnical books?
faq.answer: Yes, but the templates and examples are strongest for instructional and technical content.

faq.question: Can it check chapter dependency cycles?
faq.answer: Yes. The included validator script checks required chapter dependencies in a YAML outline.

faq.question: Does it require external services?
faq.answer: No. The skill content is local, and the helper scripts do not make network requests.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
